requirements.title: SM Root Structure
requirements.description: The state machine root shall hold a pointer to the current state.
requirements.rationale: The state machine root holds the current state pointer, which is the minimal state needed to drive a state machine without dynamic allocation or global state.

requirements.title: State Root Structure
requirements.description: Each state shall embed a module root with an API vtable, a pointer to the owning SM, and a next-state option.
requirements.rationale: Embedding a module root in each state enables polymorphic dispatch of state actions through the vtable, allowing different state implementations to share the same interface.

requirements.title: State API Interface
requirements.description: Each state API vtable shall define StateAction, ShouldExit, and ResetState operations.
requirements.rationale: StateAction, ShouldExit, and ResetState define the minimal lifecycle hooks needed to execute, terminate, and reinitialize a state, keeping the interface simple and composable.

requirements.title: SM Initialization
requirements.description: The state machine shall initialize with a start state and failure handler.
requirements.rationale: Initializing with a start state and failure handler establishes the SM in a valid, runnable configuration with diagnostic support from the first invocation.

requirements.title: State Initialization
requirements.description: A state shall initialize with a link to its owning SM, an API vtable, and an optional next state.
requirements.rationale: Linking a state to its owning SM enables the state to trigger transitions and query SM-level context without global references.

requirements.title: Get Current State
requirements.description: The state machine shall return the current state pointer.
requirements.rationale: Exposing the current state pointer allows external logic to inspect SM status, which is useful for diagnostics, telemetry, and conditional behavior.

requirements.title: State Transition
requirements.description: The state machine shall transition to the next state when the current state's next-state option is present.
requirements.rationale: Option-driven transitions decouple transition logic from the SM engine; a state signals a transition by setting the next-state option, keeping the transition protocol explicit and deterministic.

requirements.title: No Transition When None
requirements.description: The state machine shall remain in the current state when the next-state option is absent.
requirements.rationale: Remaining in the current state when no transition is requested is the safe default, preventing unintended state changes due to uninitialized option fields.

requirements.title: SM Verification
requirements.description: The state machine shall verify that its root and current state pointer are non-NULL.
requirements.rationale: Verifying root and state pointer validity at runtime ensures memory safety before the SM engine dereferences any internal pointers.

requirements.title: State Verification
requirements.description: Each state shall verify that its API vtable and owning SM pointer are non-NULL.
requirements.rationale: Validating each state's vtable and owning-SM pointer at runtime catches misconfigured states before they produce undefined behavior through invalid dispatch or null dereference.

requirements.title: Next State Pointer Verification
requirements.description: Each state shall verify that the next-state pointer is non-NULL when the next-state option is marked present.
requirements.rationale: A present option with a NULL pointer would cause a null dereference during transition; validating this precondition prevents that undefined behavior.
